visual_mood: Clear visual explainer showing how polarized lenses block horizontal glare -- simple diagram with before/after eye comfort

layout.composition: Top: headline question. Middle: illustrated diagram showing sunlight hitting a surface, horizontal glare waves blocked by a polarized lens filter, vertical light passing through. Bottom: key takeaway text. Logo bottom-right.
layout.text_hierarchy: Headline largest, diagram labels medium, takeaway text medium, logo small
layout.background: Light sky blue gradient, clean and educational

text_elements.content: Polarized lenses block scattered horizontal light that causes glare, letting only clean vertical light reach your eyes.

product.placement: Small product shot in the bottom-left corner as a 'featured in' element -- not the hero of this image
product.instruction: The sunglasses should look like a real pair photographed in this scene -- matching the style shown in the reference image. Not a digital composite or 3D render.

brand.color_scheme: Light sky blue background, headline in black, labels in dark blue, diagram arrows in red (#E31E24) for glare and green for clean light

prompt: An educational infographic on a light sky blue gradient background explaining how polarization works. All text uses the bold italic sporty typeface from the font reference image. Large headline 'HOW DOES POLARIZATION WORK?' at top in black. Center shows a clear diagram: sunlight hitting a surface creates horizontal glare waves (shown in red), a polarized filter in the middle blocks them, and clean vertical light passes through to an eye icon on the right. Labels 'HORIZONTAL GLARE', 'POLARIZED FILTER', and 'CLEAR VISION' mark each section. A short explanation line at the bottom. Rasta Brown sunglasses from DuberyMNL sit small in the bottom-left as a product callout -- real matte sunglasses matching the reference. DuberyMNL logo bottom-right. 4:5 aspect ratio.
